015 * limitations under the License.

017package org.apache.juneau.rest.client;
018
019import java.io.*;
020
021/**
022 * SPI for pluggable HTTP transports.
023 *
024 * <p>
025 * An {@link HttpTransport} is responsible for executing a fully-resolved {@link TransportRequest} and returning a
026 * {@link TransportResponse}.  Implementations include:
027 * <ul>
028 *    <li>{@code ApacheHc45Transport} (module {@code juneau-rest-client-apache-httpclient-45})
029 *    <li>{@code ApacheHc5Transport} (module {@code juneau-rest-client-apache-httpclient-50})
030 *    <li>{@code JavaHttpTransport} (module {@code juneau-rest-client-java-httpclient})
031 *    <li>{@code OkHttpTransport} (module {@code juneau-rest-client-okhttp})
032 *    <li>{@code JettyHttpTransport} (module {@code juneau-rest-client-jetty})
033 *    <li>{@code MockHttpTransport} (module {@code juneau-rest-mock})
034 * </ul>
035 *
036 * <p>
037 * Implementations are discovered automatically via {@link HttpTransportProvider} using {@link java.util.ServiceLoader},
038 * or set explicitly via {@code RestClient.Builder#transport(HttpTransport)}.
039 *
040 * <p>
041 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
042 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
043 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
044 * (and possibly earlier).
045 *
046 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
047 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/NextGenRestClient">juneau-ng REST client</a>
048 * </ul>
049 *
050 * @since 9.2.1
051 */
052public interface HttpTransport extends Closeable {
053
054   /**
055    * Executes the given request and returns the response.
056    *
057    * <p>
058    * The caller is responsible for closing the returned {@link TransportResponse} after consuming the body.
059    *
060    * @param request The fully-resolved request. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
061    * @return The response. Never <jk>null</jk>.
062    * @throws TransportException If a network-level error occurs.
063    */
064   TransportResponse execute(TransportRequest request) throws TransportException;
065
066   /**
067    * Releases resources held by this transport (connection pools, threads, etc.).
068    *
069    * <p>
070    * The default implementation is a no-op.
071    */
072   @Override
073   default void close() throws IOException {
074      // no-op by default
075   }
076}
